nodes.label: AI as literature discovery tool
nodes.label_negative: AI as sole source for literature review
nodes.grounding_span: I would use AI suggestions as a springboard to do my own research

nodes.label: AI as explanation aid for known concepts
nodes.label_negative: AI as primary teacher for unknown concepts
nodes.grounding_span: I would already have some understanding so the AI just helped clear things up

nodes.label: AI as coding assistant
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable coder with outdated knowledge
nodes.grounding_span: if it could teach me to code in new environments what I wanted perfectly

nodes.label: AI reliability and honesty
nodes.grounding_span: a truly strong AI tool that would be incredibly useful for researchers must be more honest

nodes.label: Researcher responsibility for core work
nodes.label_negative: Delegation of core research to AI
nodes.grounding_span: I feel like that is my responsibility and I would not want to rely on an AI model to do my job

nodes.label: Epistemic accuracy and understanding
nodes.grounding_span: I would verify that the papers it suggested were real, relevant and reputable

nodes.label: Research integrity and accuracy

nodes.label: Skill development and learning

nodes.label: Cautious reliance on AI
nodes.grounding_span: I would never solely use AI for these tasks

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as a helpful tool
nodes.grounding_span: the AI just helped clear things up and explain connections
